name: Johnny Mandel
description: American composer and arranger of popular songs, film music and jazz (1925-2020)
abstract: John Alfred Mandel (November 23, 1925 – June 29, 2020) was an American composer, multi-instrumentalist, and arranger of popular songs, film music and jazz. The musicians he worked with include Count Basie, Frank Sinatra, Peggy Lee, Anita O'Day, Barbra Streisand, Tony Bennett, Diane Schuur and Shirley Horn.

interviews.title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: Rosemary Clooney and The Arrangers / A Tribute to Nelson Riddle

quotes.text: Early Hodges sounded a lot like that. You listen to Johnny Hodges on those Carnegie Hall things in the Carnegie Hall Concert with Benny [Goodman] if you didn’t know who that was it would sound a lot like Sidney except it didn't have the big vibrato.
quotes.caption: Mandel recalls Johnny Hodges' sound at a Benny Goodman concert
quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Two of my close friends, Neal Hefti, and Johnny Mandel even more than Neal because I sort of grew up with Johnny, he was my roommate on the Buddy Rich band
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: So I got this band together. We got a great book. Tadd Dameron, Johnny Mandel, Gerry Mulligan . . . who? everybody . . . Gene Roland . . . what’s the guy? some . . . all of the fine arrangers that were around.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny Mandel called me... told me that he needed some material for a new record he was producing... he wanted me to write lyrics to his old song
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: We did “The Sandpiper” with “The Shadow of Your Smile,” and that’s still a great score.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Trombones were Henry Coker, Benny Powell, and the composer-arranger Johnny Mandel.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny Mandel and I used to be roommates with Count Basie’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]
